edges.description: If AVPU level is voice, patient is considered lethargic.
edges.label: AVPU voice indicates lethargic

edges.description: If AVPU responds to pain, patient is in coma.
edges.label: AVPU pain indicates coma

edges.description: If AVPU is unresponsive, patient is in coma.
edges.label: AVPU unresponsive indicates coma

edges.description: Cold hands are required for circulatory shock assessment.
edges.label: cold hands contributes

edges.description: Capillary refill >3sec required for circulatory shock.
edges.label: delayed capillary refill

edges.description: Weak and fast pulse required for circulatory shock.
edges.label: weak fast pulse

edges.description: Diarrhea is one cause of fluid loss concern.
edges.label: diarrhea causes fluid loss concern

edges.description: Vomiting causes fluid loss concern.
edges.label: vomiting causes fluid loss concern

edges.description: Drinking poorly indicates fluid loss concern.
edges.label: drinking poorly causes fluid loss concern
edges.relationLabel: drinking poorly causes fluid loss concern

edges.description: Repeated vomiting indicates fluid loss concern.
edges.label: repeated vomiting causes fluid loss concern
edges.relationLabel: repeated vomiting causes fluid loss concern

edges.description: Lethargic patient is considered lethargic or unconscious.
edges.label: lethargic contributes to lethargic or unconscious
edges.relationLabel: lethargic contributes to lethargic or unconscious

edges.description: Comatose patient is considered lethargic or unconscious.
edges.label: coma contributes to lethargic or unconscious
edges.relationLabel: coma contributes to lethargic or unconscious

edges.description: Contributes to the dehydration sign count; if total >=2, severe dehydration is indicated.
edges.label: lethargic/unconscious is a dehydration sign

edges.description: Contributes to dehydration sign count; if total >=2, severe dehydration.
edges.label: sunken eyes is a dehydration sign
edges.relationLabel: sunken eyes is a dehydration sign

edges.description: Contributes to dehydration sign count; if total >=2, severe dehydration.
edges.label: slow skin pinch is a dehydration sign
edges.relationLabel: slow skin pinch is a dehydration sign

edges.description: Fluid loss concern is necessary for moderate dehydration.
edges.label: fluid loss concern required

edges.description: Severe dehydration must be absent for moderate dehydration.
edges.label: absence of severe dehydration

edges.description: If dehydration sign count is exactly 1, moderate dehydration is indicated.
edges.label: count=1 triggers moderate dehydration

edges.description: Drinking poorly alone can trigger moderate dehydration.
edges.label: drinking poorly triggers moderate dehydration

edges.description: Repeated vomiting alone can trigger moderate dehydration.
edges.label: repeated vomiting triggers moderate dehydration

edges.confidenceReason: Contributes to the count used in the threshold.

edges.description: Presence of respiratory distress is required for non-severe respiratory distress.
edges.label: respiratory distress present

edges.label: not severe respiratory distress

edges.description: This sign, when present, causes emergency signs to be detected.
edges.label: contributes to emergency signs detection

edges.description: This sign contributes to detection.
edges.label: contributes to emergency signs detection

edges.description: Contributes.
edges.label: contributes to emergency signs detection

edges.confidenceReason: If the list is non‑empty, priority signs detected.
edges.description: Presence of this sign means priority signs are detected.
edges.label: contributes to priority detection

edges.description: If any emergency sign is detected, patient is assigned emergency category.
edges.label: presence of emergency signs leads to emergency category
edges.relationLabel: presence of emergency signs leads to emergency category

edges.description: If any priority sign is detected (and no emergency signs), assign priority category.
edges.label: presence of priority signs leads to priority category
edges.relationLabel: presence of priority signs leads to priority category

edges.description: If no emergency or priority signs detected, assign non‑urgent category.
edges.label: absence of signs leads to non‑urgent category
edges.relationLabel: absence of signs leads to non‑urgent category

edges.description: When emergency signs are present, call a senior health worker.
edges.label: emergency signs require calling senior health worker
edges.relationLabel: emergency signs require calling senior health worker
edges.sourceText: if (emergencySignList a).length > 0 then ... [EmergencyAction.callSenior, EmergencyAction.labInvestigations]; theorem emergency_implies_call_senior_and_labs

edges.description: When emergency signs present, carry out emergency lab investigations.
edges.label: emergency signs require emergency lab investigations
edges.relationLabel: emergency signs require emergency lab investigations

edges.description: Emergency category patients must be reassessed frequently.
edges.label: emergency signs require frequent reassessment
edges.sourceText: reassessFrequently := true in emergency branch; theorem emergency_implies_reassess

edges.description: If airway obstructed, start airway management.
edges.label: airway obstruction requires start airway management
edges.relationLabel: airway obstruction requires start airway management

edges.description: Central cyanosis triggers airway management.
edges.label: central cyanosis requires start airway management
edges.relationLabel: central cyanosis requires start airway management

edges.description: If not breathing, start airway management.
edges.label: not breathing requires start airway management
edges.relationLabel: not breathing requires start airway management

edges.description: Start breathing support if severe respiratory distress present.
edges.label: severe respiratory distress requires breathing support
edges.relationLabel: severe respiratory distress requires breathing support

edges.description: Start circulatory support if shock present.
edges.label: circulatory shock requires circulatory support

edges.description: Manage coma if patient is in coma.
edges.label: coma requires coma management

edges.description: Manage convulsions if patient is convulsing.
edges.label: convulsing requires convulsion management

edges.description: Manage dehydration if severe dehydration present.
edges.label: severe dehydration requires dehydration management

edges.description: Non‑urgent patients can wait their turn in the queue.
edges.label: non‑urgent patients wait in queue
edges.sourceText: PDF: 'can wait their turn in the queue'

edges.description: Priority patients are to be placed at the front of the queue.
edges.label: priority patients placed at front of queue
edges.relationLabel: priority patients placed at front of queue
edges.sourceText: PDF: 'should be given priority in the queue'

metadata.domainPolicy: domain-agnostic extraction; no document-specific relation schema is hardcoded
metadata.edgePolicy: edges are human-readable relations between events
metadata.nodePolicy: nodes are human-readable events, states, actions, conditions, decisions, or outcomes

nodes.description: Patient's airway is obstructed.
nodes.label: airway is obstructed

nodes.description: Patient shows central cyanosis (blue skin/mucous membranes).
nodes.label: central cyanosis present

nodes.description: Patient is not breathing.
nodes.label: patient is not breathing

nodes.description: Patient has severe respiratory distress.
nodes.label: severe respiratory distress

nodes.confidenceReason: Derived from warmHands field used in hasCirculatoryShock.

nodes.label: patient has cold hands

nodes.description: Capillary refill time is greater than 3 seconds.
nodes.label: capillary refill > 3 seconds

nodes.description: Patient's pulse is weak and fast.
nodes.label: pulse is weak and fast

nodes.confidenceReason: From AVPULevel inductive and TriageAssessment field.

nodes.label: severe pain

nodes.label: restless, irritable, or lethargic

nodes.confidenceReason: Used directly in priority sign list generation.
nodes.description: Patient is under 2 months old.
nodes.label: patient age is less than 2 months

nodes.description: Patient responds to voice but not fully alert (V on AVPU).
nodes.label: patient is lethargic
nodes.sourceText: def isLethargic (avpu : AVPULevel) : Bool := avpu == AVPULevel.voice

nodes.description: Patient responds only to pain or is unresponsive (P or U on AVPU).
nodes.label: patient is in coma
nodes.sourceText: def isComa (avpu : AVPULevel) : Bool := avpu == AVPULevel.pain || avpu == AVPULevel.unresponsive

nodes.description: Patient has circulatory shock: cold hands, capillary refill >3sec, weak and fast pulse.
nodes.label: circulatory shock present
nodes.sourceText: def hasCirculatoryShock (a : TriageAssessment) : Bool := (! a.warmHands) && a.capillaryRefillOver3sec && a.weakAndFastPulse

nodes.description: Patient has diarrhea, vomiting, drinking poorly, or repeated vomiting.
nodes.label: fluid loss concern
nodes.sourceText: def fluidLossConcern (a : TriageAssessment) : Bool := a.diarrhea || a.vomiting || a.drinkingPoorly || a.repeatedVomiting

nodes.description: Patient is either lethargic (V) or unconscious (P or U).
nodes.label: patient is lethargic or unconscious
nodes.sourceText: let lethargicOrUnconscious := isComa a.avpu || isLethargic a.avpu

nodes.description: Patient has severe dehydration: dehydration sign count >= 2.
nodes.label: severe dehydration present
nodes.sourceText: def severeDehydrationPresent (a : TriageAssessment) : Bool := dehydrationSignCount a ≥ 2

nodes.description: Patient has moderate dehydration: fluid loss concern, no severe dehydration, and (count=1 or drinking poorly or repeated vomiting).
nodes.label: moderate dehydration present
nodes.sourceText: def moderateDehydrationPresent (a : TriageAssessment) : Bool := fluidLossConcern a && ! severeDehydrationPresent a && ((dehydrationSignCount a == 1) || a.drinkingPoorly || a.repeatedVomiting)

nodes.confidenceReason: Explicitly used in moderateDehydrationPresent; threshold logic.
nodes.description: Exactly one of lethargic/unconscious, sunken eyes, or slow skin pinch is true.
nodes.label: dehydration sign count equals 1

nodes.description: Patient has respiratory distress but it is not severe.
nodes.label: respiratory distress but not severe

nodes.description: Severe dehydration is NOT present.
nodes.label: no severe dehydration

nodes.confidenceReason: Used in condition for priority respiratory distress.
